caseStudies.title: Prescription Hope
caseStudies.subtitle: Healthcare payment platform processing $10M+/month for 90K+ patients
caseStudies.problem: A healthcare platform serving 90K+ patients needed reliable multi-gateway recurring billing, complex renewal workflows, and a backend sustaining $10M+ monthly payment volume — with zero tolerance for downtime or data inconsistency across three different payment processors.
caseStudies.solution: Owned the full backend across employee and patient portals. Built a unified payment abstraction layer over Stripe, USAePay, and Payroc with automated retry logic. Designed 20+ cron jobs for billing cycles, renewals, and failure recovery. Optimized the database layer with strategic indexing for sub-100ms query performance at scale.
architecture: Laravel monolith serving dual portals (employee + patient)
Multi-gateway payment abstraction layer (Stripe, USAePay, Payroc)
Automated retry and reconciliation logic across processors
MySQL with strategic indexing and optimized query plans
20+ cron jobs for billing automation, renewals, retries
AWS infrastructure (EC2, RDS, S3)
caseStudies.impact: Made the billing engine the most reliable part of the stack — zero payment processing downtime, with automated retry logic handling edge cases across three different payment processors.
caseStudies.founderTakeaway: If you're processing payments at scale, I've already solved this problem.

caseStudies.title: Veva Collect
caseStudies.subtitle: Distributed file pipeline handling 5K+ daily uploads for Grammy-winning producers
caseStudies.problem: A music collaboration platform used by major labels and Grammy-winning producers was failing under ~5,000 daily uploads (files up to 10GB), slow ZIP generation for 25GB+ multi-file shares, and outages during peak concurrent usage.
caseStudies.solution: Designed a distributed file-processing pipeline using AWS S3, SQS, Lambda, Redis, and Go microservices. Built an async ZIP creation system handling 25GB+ payloads without blocking the UX. Introduced load balancing to eliminate outages during heavy collaboration sessions.
architecture: Laravel API + Go microservices for file processing
AWS S3 with multipart uploads for large object storage
SQS + Lambda for async event-driven processing
Redis for caching and job state management
GraphQL API layer for flexible client queries
Load balancer for high-concurrency traffic distribution
caseStudies.impact: Transformed an unreliable file-heavy platform into a robust event-driven system. Eliminated upload-related outages and made 25GB+ file sharing seamless for Grammy-winning producers and major label teams.
caseStudies.founderTakeaway: If your app handles heavy file processing or needs event-driven architecture, I've done this at scale.

caseStudies.title: Aldawli ERP
caseStudies.subtitle: Supply-chain attack recovery on a live enterprise ERP — zero downtime
caseStudies.problem: A production ERP system handling enterprise inventory, procurement, and financials was silently compromised by a malicious third-party Composer dependency — containing obfuscated base64 payloads, eval() execution, hidden admin routes bypassing authentication, and data exfiltration behavior. The system appeared stable; the threat was invisible at the application layer.
caseStudies.solution: Identified the supply-chain compromise during a dependency audit. Isolated the malicious package's execution paths without disrupting the live system. Conducted full dependency chain analysis, assessed system-wide exposure, cloned and manually sanitized the package, then revalidated every dependent module before redeployment — all within 30 hours, with zero downtime.
architecture: Laravel-based ERP (inventory, procurement, financials)
Compromised dependency isolation and execution path severance
Full dependency chain analysis and exposure assessment
Manual package sanitization against known-good references
Module-by-module revalidation before production redeployment
Post-incident audit and access control hardening
caseStudies.impact: Restored a silently compromised production ERP to a fully verified secure state within 30 hours — no downtime, no data loss, and every execution path the malicious package touched was individually validated.
caseStudies.founderTakeaway: If your production system has ever felt 'too stable to worry about,' it might be time for a dependency audit.

caseStudies.title: Step2Compliance
caseStudies.subtitle: Cross-platform async pipeline for .xlsm generation — 65% faster delivery
caseStudies.problem: A compliance platform required reliable generation of .xlsm (macro-enabled Excel) files — a format that cannot be natively produced in Linux environments. The existing synchronous approach blocked the UI and caused poor performance under load.
caseStudies.solution: Designed an asynchronous, cross-platform pipeline: Laravel dispatches jobs to a queue, a Python service running on a dedicated Windows EC2 instance handles .xlsm generation, files land in S3, and webhook callbacks plus WebSocket notifications close the loop — keeping the main application fully responsive.
architecture: Laravel backend with queue-based job dispatching
Windows EC2 instance for platform-specific .xlsm generation
Python service for file processing on the Windows compute layer
AWS S3 for file storage and delivery
Webhook-based completion callbacks to Laravel
WebSocket notifications for real-time client updates
caseStudies.impact: Eliminated UI blocking entirely and improved file delivery time by 65% — turning an unreliable synchronous process into a production-grade cross-platform pipeline.
caseStudies.founderTakeaway: If you need platform-specific processing without compromising your main stack, I've built the decoupled architecture for it.

caseStudies.title: RadioBridge IoT Analytics
caseStudies.subtitle: Real-time analytics dashboards over LoRaWAN sensor telemetry
caseStudies.problem: An IoT company collecting LoRaWAN sensor data had no real-time analytics or visualization — stakeholders had zero visibility into device performance and network health.
caseStudies.solution: Built analytics dashboards for real-time IoT data visualization, backend integrations for ingesting LoRaWAN telemetry, and data pipelines transforming raw sensor data into actionable insights.
architecture: Laravel backend for API and data processing
Real-time data ingestion from LoRaWAN devices
Analytics dashboard with time-series visualization
ETL pipeline for sensor data transformation
caseStudies.impact: Delivered the company's first real-time visibility into device performance and network health, enabling data-driven decisions on device uptime and network operations.
caseStudies.founderTakeaway: If you need data pipelines and analytics dashboards built on real-time sensor data, I know this space.

caseStudies.title: GFS — Odyssey Group
caseStudies.subtitle: Multi-tenant insurance platform serving NFIP Direct Flood, Farmers Insurance, and others
caseStudies.problem: A multi-tenant insurance management platform serving multiple large insurance clients including NFIP Direct Flood and Farmers Insurance required Laravel upgrades, security hardening, and module redesigns across isolated client environments — all without disrupting live insurance workflows.
caseStudies.solution: Worked as a fullstack developer maintaining and improving the platform. Upgraded multiple Laravel repositories to Laravel 12, elevated PHPStan static analysis from Level 1 to Level 5, resolved Aikido-flagged SQL injection vulnerabilities, and redesigned insurance endorsement modules across multiple client programs.
architecture: Multi-tenant Laravel application with subdomain-based client isolation
React.js frontend per tenant environment
Laravel 12 across multiple upgraded repositories
PHPStan Level 5 static analysis enforcement
Aikido security scanning integration
caseStudies.impact: Modernized a production multi-tenant insurance platform to Laravel 12, significantly improved code quality and type safety through PHPStan enforcement, and eliminated flagged security vulnerabilities across all client environments.
caseStudies.founderTakeaway: If your multi-tenant platform is running on outdated Laravel or has unresolved security flags, I've done exactly this modernization in production.
